revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:厳嶋神社 (新宿区)]]


{{Source citation|date=January 2025}}
{{infobox religious building
 | name = Itsukushima Shrine (Shinjuku Ward)
 | native_name = 厳嶋神社
 | religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|en|Shinto|qid=Q812767|lt=Shinto|1=Shinto}}
 | image = File:Nuke-Benten.jpg
 | address = {{ill|Tokyo|en|Tokyo|qid=Q1490|lt=Tokyo|1=Tokyo}}{{ill|Shinjuku|en|Shinjuku|qid=Q179645|lt=Shinjuku Ward|1=Shinjuku}}{{ill|Yochōmachi|ja|余丁町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yochōmachi|1=Yochōmachi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11385244}}8-5
 | location = {{Wiki coordinates 2-level|35|41|51|N|139|42|48|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}}
 | enshrined_deity = {{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|イチキシマヒメ|1=Ichikishima-hime|lt=Ichikishima-hime|lt=Ichikishima-hime|lt_ja=市杵島姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|qq=}} ({{ill|Benzaiten|ja|弁財天|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Benzaiten|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 弁才天}})
 | founded = {{ill|1086|en|1086|qid=Q19460|lt=1086|1=1086}} ({{ill|Ōtoku|en|Ōtoku|qid=Q264214|lt=Outoku|1=Ōtoku}} 3)
 | alternate_name = Nukebenzaiten (抜弁天)
 | status = Former Unclassified Shrine
 | associated_site = {{ill|Shinjuku Yamanote Seven Lucky Gods|ja|新宿山ノ手七福神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinjuku Yamanote Seven Lucky Gods|1=Shinjuku Yamanote Seven Lucky Gods|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q20043075}}
 | festival = September 5
| location = {{ill|Shinjuku|qid=Q179645|1=Shinjuku}}
 | coordinates = {{Coord|35.6975|N|139.71333333|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}
 }}

'''Itsukushima Shrine''' (厳嶋神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=Shinto Shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Tokyo|en|Tokyo|qid=Q1490|lt=Tokyo|1=Tokyo}}{{ill|Shinjuku|en|Shinjuku|qid=Q179645|lt=Shinjuku Ward|1=Shinjuku}}{{ill|Yochōmachi|ja|余丁町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yochōmachi|1=Yochōmachi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11385244}}. The enshrined deity is {{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|イチキシマヒメ|1=Ichikishima-hime|lt=Ichikishima-hime|lt=Ichikishima-hime|lt_ja=市杵島姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|qq=}}, but due to its syncretism with {{ill|Buddhism|en|Buddhism|qid=Q748|lt=Buddhism|1=Buddhism}}’s {{ill|Benzaiten|en|Benzaiten|qid=Q818468|qid=Q818468|lt=Benzaiten|1=Benzaiten}}, it is known as a shrine that venerates the {{ill|Benzaiten|en|Benzaiten|qid=Q818468|qid=Q818468|lt=Benzaiten|1=Benzaiten}} of the {{ill|Shinjuku Yamanote Seven Lucky Gods|ja|新宿山ノ手七福神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinjuku Yamanote Seven Lucky Gods|1=Shinjuku Yamanote Seven Lucky Gods|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q20043075}}. Its common name is '''Nukebenzaiten''' (抜弁天).

In connection with the shrine, the nearby street is named {{ill|Nukebenzaiten Street|ja|抜弁天通り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nukebenzaiten Street|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 東京都道302号新宿両国線|1=Tokyo Metropolitan Road Route 302|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11102993}}.

== History ==
{{ill|1086|en|1086|qid=Q19460|lt=1086|1=1086}} ({{ill|Ōtoku|en|Ōtoku|qid=Q264214|lt=Outoku|1=Ōtoku}} 3) saw {{ill|Chinjufu shōgun|en|Chinjufu shōgun|qid=Q2700052|lt=Chinju-fu Shōgun|1=Chinjufu shōgun}} {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshiie|en|Minamoto no Yoshiie|qid=Q32613|lt=Minamoto no Yoshiie|1=Minamoto no Yoshiie}} (Hachiman Tarō) camp here during the {{ill|Gosannen War|en|Gosannen War|qid=Q1187411|lt=Gosannen War|1=Gosannen War}} (1083–1087) on his way to pacify {{ill|Oshu|en|Oshu|qid=Q1175530|lt=Mutsu Province|1=Oshu}}. This location, being the highest point in the area and once affording views of {{ill|Mount Fuji|en|Mount Fuji|qid=Q39231|lt=Mount Fuji|1=Mount Fuji}}, inspired Yoshiie to gaze into the distance toward Mount Fuji and further, to pray for victory at the shrine of {{ill|Itsukushima Shrine|en|Itsukushima Shrine|qid=Q191763|lt=Itsukushima Shrine|1=Itsukushima Shrine}} enshrined in {{ill|Aki Province|en|Aki Province|qid=Q965029|lt=Aki Province|1=Aki Province}}.

After achieving the pacification of Oshu, Yoshiie is said to have established a shrine here on his return journey to give thanks for his victory by enshrining Itsukushima Shrine through {{ill|Bunrei|en|Bunrei|qid=Q195793|lt=Enshrinement|1=Bunrei}}.

Legends tell that Yoshiie paused at this site to pray and overcome hardships, and because the shrine’s approach runs north to south, it came to be known as "Nukebenzaiten", one of Edo’s six Benzaitens.

During the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|qid=Q184963|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period}}, there was also an {{ill|Inari shrine|en|Inari shrine|qid=Q514480|lt=Inari Shrine|1=Inari shrine}}, and under {{ill|Tokugawa Tsunayoshi|en|Tokugawa Tsunayoshi|qid=Q313745|lt=Tokugawa Tsunayoshi|1=Tokugawa Tsunayoshi}}’s {{ill|Edicts on Compassion for Living Things|ja|生類憐れみの令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Edicts on Compassion for Living Things|1=Shōrui Awaremi no Rei|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q10855187}}, a 25,000-tsubo doghouse for stray dogs was established in the vicinity, known as {{ill|Doghouse (Edo Shogunate)|ja|犬小屋 (Tokugawa shogunate)|lt=Doghouse|lt_ja=犬小屋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Doghouse (Edo Shogunate)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.

== address ==
*{{ill|Tokyo|en|Tokyo|qid=Q1490|lt=Tokyo|1=Tokyo}}{{ill|Shinjuku|en|Shinjuku|qid=Q179645|lt=Shinjuku Ward|1=Shinjuku}}{{ill|Yochōmachi|ja|余丁町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yochōmachi|1=Yochōmachi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11385244}}8-5
**Toei Oedo Line “{{ill|Higashi-shinjuku Station|en|Higashi-shinjuku Station|qid=Q112503|lt=Higashi-Shinjuku|1=Higashi-shinjuku Station}}”, Toei Bus “Nukebenzaiten” (get off)


== See Also ==
